checks.finding: REQ-001..004 each state observable pass/fail conditions: REQ-001's fail-closed exit-code/no-mutation behavior is measured by AC-001..006 (exit codes, git status --porcelain, grep self-checks, line-number ordering); REQ-002's required job/needs wiring is measured by AC-007..010 (text-marker checks on release.yml, including a negative-branch canary AC-009); REQ-003's doc/CHANGELOG delivery is measured by AC-011/012; REQ-004's no-.ps1-twin decision is measured by AC-013 (non-existence assertion) and AC-014 (doc content marker). No in-scope requirement lacks a concrete downstream validation hook.

The four Goals-section items (REQ-001..004) each have an explicit '(REQ-00N)' tag on one or more of AC-001..014, and acceptance-tests.md's table confirms the same REQ-to-AC-to-TEST mapping with no orphaned goal or untraced AC.

All 14 ACs describe externally observable artifacts: process exit codes, `git status --porcelain` output, grep/text-marker substrings inside real source files, line-number comparisons, and file-existence checks. None rely on vague intent language such as 'works correctly' or 'is robust'.

The Non-goals section explicitly excludes six adjacent expansion vectors that this feature could otherwise be pulled into: the broader risk-adaptive-layer release-gating scope (INV-015), suite-budget harmonization (OQ-005), emitting timing data into release notes/attestation (OQ-006), authoring bump-version.ps1 (REQ-004/OQ-003), broadening release.yml's trigger surface, and touching protected-gate files — each with a specific reason or cross-reference.

Material constraints are stated: no-bypass fail-closed behavior (AC-004, OQ-007 resolution), no weakened-gate escape hatch on the CI leg (AC-008/AC-009), a Security Boundaries table (B1-B4) covering fixture-vs-real-repo isolation and data classification, an explicit cross-host/compatibility degradation (REQ-004, bash-only with no .ps1 twin), and an approval boundary in Roles and Permissions (human maintainer approves spec/tasks and publishes releases; agent edits are confirmed non-protected via INV-005).

The Risks section's High item (silent future bypass addition) names AC-004 and AC-008/AC-009 as the locked, reviewable validation surface; its Medium item (fixture $ROOT-path resolution correctness) names AC-001's happy-path assertion as the canary. Both high/medium-risk claims have a concrete planned validation surface within this spec's own acceptance criteria, so the check applies and passes rather than needing SKIP.
